heading: Shard a Global Collection for Global Writes in the Atlas UI
body:
> **Note:** 
>
> - Global writes are only supported on global clusters, which
>   require an `M30+` cluster tier.
>
> - When Global Writes are enabled, Global Clusters do not support dedicated search nodes.
>
> - You can use the Atlas UI to shard a global collection only
>   for global clusters with [Atlas-Managed Sharding](atlas/tutorial/create-global-cluster)
>   enabled.
>
> - This document assumes prior knowledge of sharding semantics.
>   Review the [Sharding documentation](manual/sharding) before
>   continuing with this tutorial.

In sharded clusters, you can create [zones](manual/reference/glossary) of sharded
data based on the [shard keys](manual/reference/glossary). Your zones can segment data
based on geographic area. To learn more about sharding based
on geographic area, see [Segmenting Data by Location](manual/tutorial/sharding-segmenting-data-by-location).

The Atlas UI supports sharding Global Write collections
for only global clusters that use [Atlas-Managed Sharding](atlas/tutorial/create-global-cluster).
To shard non-Global-Write collections or Global Write collections
that use Self-Managed Sharding, you must use [mongosh](mongodb-shell) or application
code. To learn more, see [Deploy a Sharded Cluster](manual/tutorial/deploy-shard-cluster).
